beats.text: A forest fills the frame — trees, canopy, soil, the works. That's an ecosystem.
Zoom in. One organism: a student walking through. Zoom further — an eye, then a single photoreceptor cell inside it.
Deeper still: ribosomes, tiny molecular machines, assembling proteins inside that one cell.
Life is nested — ecosystem, organism, organ, cell, molecule. Each level stacks perfectly inside the next.
Here's the wild part — a timeline appears, stretching back three point seven billion years. Bacteria from that era run the same molecular rules as that cell.
Multicellularity erupts at six hundred million years, and diversity explodes. Master the molecular level — you hold the key to all of it.
